title: empetrum eamesii subsp. hermaphroditum
intro: Empetrum eamesii subsp. hermaphroditum, commonly known as the twinflower or mountain crowberry, is a subspecies of the empetrum plant, which is a type of flowering shrub native to the Arctic regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Empetrum eamesii subsp. hermaphroditum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or by using it as a tincture, and it is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves for topical application.
